---
applyTo: "**/*.go"
---

# General Coding Conventions

## Module and Package Layout

- Module: `github.com/tomtwinkle/es-typed-go`
- `estype/` — core types shared across ES versions (`Field`, `Index`, `Alias`, `DateFormat`, mapping parser)
- `esv8/` — Elasticsearch v8 client wrapper and property builders
- `esv9/` — Elasticsearch v9 client wrapper and property builders
- `esv8/query/`, `esv9/query/` — query/sort/aggregation builders
- `cmd/estyped/` — CLI code generator that produces typed `Field` constants from ES mapping JSON

## Type Safety

- Use `estype.Field` (not `string`) for every Elasticsearch field-name parameter.
- Use `estype.Index` (not `string`) for index names; `estype.Alias` for alias names.
- These distinct types prevent mix-ups at compile time, similar to how sqlc generates typed SQL access.

## Error Handling

- Wrap errors with `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)`.
- Public functions that can fail return `(T, error)` — error is always the last return value.
- Parse/convert functions follow the pattern `ParseXxx(input) (T, error)`.

## Logging

- Use the standard `log/slog` package for structured logging; never use `fmt.Println` for log output.

## Functional Options Pattern

- Property constructors follow: `NewXxxProperty(opts ...XxxPropertyOption) types.XxxProperty`.
- Callers can supply anonymous functions as options: `func(p *types.XxxProperty) { p.Field = val }`.

## Fluent Builders

- Method chaining is confined to the `query` sub-packages (`esv8/query`, `esv9/query`).
- Every builder exposes a `Build()` method to return the final value.

## Code Generation

- Generated files begin with `// Code generated by estyped; DO NOT EDIT.`.
- Regenerate with `go generate ./...` (uses `//go:generate go run ./generator` directives in package docs).
- The `cmd/estyped` CLI accepts `-mapping`, `-out`, `-package`, and optionally `-name` flags.

## Interface Design

- `ESClient` exposes a curated subset of ES operations.
- `ESClientSpec` covers the full ES API spec (generated).
- Index-oriented methods receive `estype.Index`; alias-oriented methods receive `estype.Alias`.

## Versioning

- Keep `esv8/` and `esv9/` in sync: any change to one usually needs the same change in the other.
- Import the correct ES typed-client package: `github.com/elastic/go-elasticsearch/v8` or `.../v9`.

## Documentation Style

- Do not use emoji in documentation or comments.
- Do not use excessive bold for inline emphasis within sentences. Bold is reserved for list-item headings (e.g., `- **Name** — description`) and table headers.
- Keep a neutral, factual tone in README and doc comments.
